Migrate authentication from next-auth to better-auth (#446)

* feat: migrate authentication from next-auth to better-auth

Replace next-auth v4 with better-auth for all authentication flows
(email/password and GitHub OAuth). Uses a custom Knex adapter to
map better-auth's schema onto existing database tables, preserving
all user data and bcrypt password hashes without data migration.

- Add better-auth server instance with custom Knex adapter
- Add database migration for better-auth required columns
- Update all ~20 server pages to use new session helper
- Convert sign-in/signup to client-side auth via better-auth/react
- Update guards (AuthenticatedGuard, OAuthGuard) for new session API
- Remove next-auth, storageAdapter, and related utilities

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(auth): correct knex adapter transform and baseURL scheme

- Rewrite knexAdapter to return raw DB rows; factory handles all
  field name transformation via createAdapterFactory, eliminating
  double-transform that caused credential sign-in to fail
- Fix baseURL to always include https:// scheme since config.host
  is stored as a plain hostname without protocol

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(db): add ipAddress and userAgent columns to sessions table

better-auth writes these fields when creating sessions; without them
the session insert fails with a column-not-found error.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(signup): create credential provider entry on account creation

After creating a new account via /api/v1/accounts, insert a
credential provider row in account_providers so better-auth can
authenticate the new user with email/password.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor(signup): move credential provider creation into database layer

Add CreateCredentialProviderParams type and createCredentialProvider
method to AccountDatabase interface and SQL implementation, replacing
the direct knex usage in the accounts route.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address PR review issues

- knexAdapter: fix update re-fetch to use ID after updating, so token
  rotation (WHERE token=old → SET token=new) returns correct row
- knexAdapter: fix update to return null early if no row found before updating
- auth: share knex pool via memoized getKnex() in lib/database/index.ts,
  eliminating duplicate connection pool
- auth: gate debug logging to development only (warn in production)
- auth route: use toNextJsHandler from better-auth/next-js for correct
  Next.js header/response handling
- CredentialForm: wrap signIn.email() in try/catch so network errors
  reset loading state instead of leaving form stuck
- signin page: remove hardcoded hasCredentials=true dead branching logic
- AuthenticationProviders: add callbackURL='/settings' to signIn.social()
  so users return to settings after GitHub OAuth
- sessions page: check __Secure- prefixed cookie first (production), then
  unprefix fallback (development)
- account.ts: make credential provider insert atomic within createAccount
  transaction, removing the need for a separate createCredentialProvider call

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(auth): defer toNextJsHandler initialization to request time

Calling toNextJsHandler(getAuth()) at module level triggers getKnex()
during build-time page data collection, failing when DB env vars are
absent. Defer to request time; getAuth() is memoized so there's no
performance cost.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Address PR review feedback for better-auth migration

- Validate formData inputs in CredentialForm (null check, type guard)
- Sanitize redirectBack in CredentialForm and SigninButton (open redirect)
- Export AuthSession type from getActorFromSession for reuse
- Derive session cookie names from constants instead of hardcoding
- Add requireEmailVerification for credential login
- Transform data keys and where clause fields in knexAdapter
- Escape LIKE wildcard characters in knexAdapter
- Sync password hash to account_providers on change/reset
- Remove PostgreSQL-specific useTz from migration timestamps
- Reuse single Knex pool (getSQLDatabase takes Knex instance)
- Improve unlink test assertion to verify specific provider removed

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address additional PR review feedback

- Wrap migration up/down in transaction for atomicity
- Add onError handler to signOut in LogoutButton and header
- Support select parameter in knexAdapter findOne/findMany
- Guard createCredentialProvider against duplicate-key errors

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address third round of PR review feedback

- Show error alert instead of redirecting on signOut failure

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix P0: set emailVerified=true on account creation and verification

createAccount with no verification code and verifyAccount both set
verifiedAt but never set emailVerified=true, causing better-auth to
block sign-in for all new users under requireEmailVerification: true.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add onError handler for OAuth sign-in and clean up getKnex fallback

- Add onError callback to SigninButton OAuth flow for user feedback
- Remove unreachable fallback branch in getKnex(), throw if DB not init
- Revert update select (not part of better-auth adapter contract)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix knexAdapter double-transformation bug

The createAdapterFactory already transforms where clauses and data
objects (via transformWhereClause and transformInput) before passing
them to the adapter. The adapter's transformData/transformWhere
helpers were applying a second transformation, causing field name
collisions (e.g., accountId being both a schema field and DB column
for different fields). This broke credential lookup during sign-in.

Remove transformData/transformWhere from the adapter since where and
data already arrive with DB column names. Keep getFieldName only for
select and sortBy.field which the factory does not transform.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address Greptile confidence score issues

- Add comprehensive knexAdapter.test.ts with 27 tests covering all
  CRUD operations, where operators (eq, ne, in, not_in, contains,
  starts_with, ends_with), LIKE wildcard escaping, select projection,
  sortBy, offset/limit, OR connector, and cross-table operations.

- Replace alert() with inline error states in SigninButton,
  AuthenticationProviders, LogoutButton, and Header components,
  matching the pattern already used in CredentialForm.

- Make credential form conditional on sign-in page: render based on
  auth.enableCredential config (defaults to showing when no OAuth
  providers configured). Add enableCredential field to AuthConfig.

- Refactor getDatabase/getKnex in lib/database/index.ts: replace
  fragile module-level sharedKnex side-effect with a single memoized
  getDatabaseInstance() that returns both Database and Knex, making
  the initialization relationship explicit.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix actorId persistence, update scope, and credential toggle

- Declare actorId as additionalFields in session config so
  transformInput does not strip it. Without this, the databaseHooks
  session.create.before hook's actorId injection was silently dropped
  by the factory's whitelist-based field transformer, breaking
  multi-actor switching.

- Scope knexAdapter update to WHERE id = <pre-fetched-id> instead of
  the original WHERE clause, preventing unintended multi-row updates
  when the where clause matches more than one row.

- Fix credentialEnabled precedence: respect enableCredential: false
  even when no OAuth providers are configured.

- Add test for single-row update scoping.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: comprehensive self-healing for multi-agent groups lost on restart (#412)

## Problem
Multi-agent groups were silently lost across app restarts. The
`organization.json` on disk had:
- All groups with `IsMultiAgent=false`
- All sessions with `Role=Worker` (even orchestrator sessions)
- Multi-agent group IDs from event-diagnostics didn't exist in the file

Affected teams: PR Review Squad, MAUI - PR Reviewer, FixWeirdInfoPList,
PP- Organize, Evaluate Ortinau Skills, Implement & Challenge (6 teams
total, ~25 sessions).

## Root Cause
The self-healing in `LoadOrganization` only checked `Role ==
Orchestrator`, but all sessions defaulted to `Worker` when the org file
was stale. Chicken-and-egg: can't detect orchestrator → can't heal group
→ can't protect sessions → sessions scatter.

## Fix
Three-phase self-healing in `HealMultiAgentGroups()`:

1. **Phase 1**: Detect orchestrator sessions by name pattern
(`*-orchestrator*`) and restore `Role=Orchestrator`
2. **Phase 2**: Restore `IsMultiAgent` on groups that have orchestrator
sessions (with name matching to avoid incorrectly marking repo groups)
3. **Phase 3**: Reconstruct missing multi-agent groups from scattered
sessions by detecting team members via name prefix patterns

Also adds save verification and error logging to
`event-diagnostics.log`.

## Tests
6 new tests covering:
- Orchestrator role detection by name pattern (with suffix variants)
- Group reconstruction from scattered sessions
- Multiple scattered teams handling
- No false positives on regular session names
- No-op when groups are already correct

All 55 organization tests pass.

---------

commit.message:
Allow hosts key to be empty for manual labels (#42022)

<!-- Add the related story/sub-task/bug number, like Resolves #123, or
remove if NA -->
**Related issue:** Resolves #41672 

# Details

Updates GitOps label functionality so that omitting the `hosts:` key
under a manual label will _not_ clear hosts from that label, but will
instead preserve the existing membership. This allows users to manage
manual hosts with an external system (via the labels API), while still
managing the labels themselves in GitOps.

# Checklist for submitter

If some of the following don't apply, delete the relevant line.

- [X] Changes file added for user-visible changes in `changes/`,

commit.message:
Fix iOS TestFlight build: remove hardcoded signing identity, clean up plist injection (#411)

## Problem
The iOS build has been failing on TestFlight CI ([run
#23331578098](https://github.com/PureWeen/PolyPilot/actions/runs/23331578098/job/67864110605))
with:

```
Apple Development: Jakub Florkowski (8MT3Q77PP8): no identity found
error MSB3073: codesign --force --sign "Apple Development: Jakub Florkowski (8MT3Q77PP8)" ... exited with code 1.
```

## Root Cause
The csproj had a post-build target (`InjectIOSPrivacyKeys`) with a
**hardcoded codesign exec** using Jakub Florkowski's signing identity.
This was part of a workaround that re-injected NS*UsageDescription
privacy keys into Info.plist after build, then re-signed the app.

## Fix
**Removed the entire PlistBuddy injection mechanism** (both
`InjectMacCatalystPrivacyKeys` and `InjectIOSPrivacyKeys` targets + the
hardcoded codesign).

The injection was a workaround for an old MAUI behavior that stripped
`NS*UsageDescription` keys from the output Info.plist. This is no longer
the case — verified with a fully clean build (`rm -rf bin/ obj/`) on
.NET 10 that the `CompileAppManifest` task in
[`dotnet/macios`](https://github.com/dotnet/macios/blob/main/msbuild/Xamarin.MacDev.Tasks/Tasks/CompileAppManifest.cs)
now preserves all source plist keys.

The privacy keys (`NSMicrophoneUsageDescription`,
`NSSpeechRecognitionUsageDescription`) are already defined in both
`Platforms/iOS/Info.plist` and `Platforms/MacCatalyst/Info.plist` and
survive the build pipeline untouched.

## Verification
- ✅ Full clean build (bin/ + obj/ nuked) on Mac Catalyst — 0 errors
- ✅ Privacy keys present in output `.app/Contents/Info.plist` without
any injection
- ✅ Confirmed `CompileAppManifest` [source
code](https://github.com/dotnet/macios/blob/main/msbuild/Xamarin.MacDev.Tasks/Tasks/CompileAppManifest.cs#L105)
reads full source plist and doesn't strip NS* keys

---------

commit.message:
feat(omegaconf): Add deferred resolver support with double-pass resolution and custom user resolvers (#1371)

## Description

This PR re-adds and enhances the deferred resolver functionality for the
OmegaConf inventory backend, along with comprehensive tests and new
features.

### Related Issue
Fixes #

---

## Proposed Changes

### 🚀 Core Features

#### Deferred (Escaped) Resolver Support
- Implemented double-pass resolution in OmegaConf backend
- First pass resolves all interpolations; escaped ones (`\${...}`)
become unescaped
- Second pass resolves the previously escaped interpolations
- Added `process_literals()` function with `KAPITAN_LITERAL` markers to
protect interpolation syntax

#### User-Defined Custom Resolvers
- Support for custom resolvers via `inventory/resolvers.py`
- Resolvers can access `_root_` context for target-level data
- Must implement `pass_resolvers()` function returning a dict of `{name:
func}`

### 🔧 Improvements

#### Enhanced Error Handling
- Added detailed error logging with traceback in `OmegaConfInventory`
- Fixed handling of empty YAML files (`yaml.safe_load` returns `None`)
- Fixed handling of null values for `classes:`, `parameters:`,
`applications:`, and `exports:`

#### Kubernetes Resource Base Class
- New `Base` class in `lib/kubernetes/k8s.py` for Kadet components
- Helper classes: `ConfigMap`, `Secret`, `ComponentConfig`,
`ComponentSecret`
- Methods for setting API version, kind, name, labels, annotations,
namespace

---

## Files Changed

### Core Changes
- `kapitan/inventory/backends/omegaconf/__init__.py` - Double-pass
resolution, empty file handling
- `kapitan/inventory/backends/omegaconf/resolvers.py` - New resolvers,
literal marker processing

### Tests
- `tests/test_compile.py` - New `CompileTestResourcesOCOmegaconf` class
- `tests/test_inventory.py` - `InventoryTargetTestOmegaConfOC` tests
- `tests/test_omegaconf.py` - Comprehensive resolver and backend tests
(~500 new lines)

### New Test Resources (`tests/test_resources_oc/`)
- `inventory/resolvers.py` - Example custom resolvers (`get_suffix`,
`get_substr`, `mul`, `upper`, `capitalize`, `escape`, `vaultkv`,
`helm_dep`, `helm_input`)
- `inventory/targets/` - Test targets: `test-deferred.yml`,
`test-merge.yml`, `test-resolvers.yml`
- `inventory/classes/components/` - Test classes for deferred
evaluation, merging, and resolver functionality
- `lib/kubernetes/` - Kadet base classes for Kubernetes resources

---

## Usage Example

### Deferred Resolution
```yaml
parameters:
  deployment_template:
    name: \${parentkey:}  # Deferred - resolves to key name when merged
    
  deployments:
    web-app: ${merge:${deployment_template}, {replicas: 2}}
    # Result: name will be "web-app"
```

### Custom Resolvers (`inventory/resolvers.py`)
```python
def get_suffix(name: str):
    return name.split("-")[-1]

def pass_resolvers():
    return {"get_suffix": get_suffix}
```

### Conditional Logic
```yaml
parameters:
  feature_enabled: true
  config: ${if:${feature_enabled}, {key: value}}
```

---

## Docs and Tests

- [x] Tests added
- [x] Updated documentation

---

## Checklist

- [x] Deferred resolver functionality works with double-pass resolution
- [x] Custom user resolvers can be loaded from `inventory/resolvers.py`
- [x] Empty YAML files and null values are handled gracefully
- [x] New conditional resolvers (`if`, `ifelse`, `and`, `or`, `not`,
`equal`)
- [x] Comprehensive test coverage for all new features

---------

commit.message:
Fix #749: Enable on-demand creation of reference dependencies (#1389)

- Modified reveal() function in kapitan/refs/functions.py to
automatically create missing references that have generation functions
when they are revealed by another reference
- This solves the dependency ordering problem where public keys (using
||reveal:path|publickey) failed when the private key (||rsa or
||ed25519) didn't exist yet
- The fix works recursively for multi-level dependency chains

Changes:
- kapitan/refs/functions.py: Enhanced reveal() with RefFromFuncError
handling for on-demand reference creation, comprehensive docstring with
examples
- tests/test_refs.py: Added 4 comprehensive tests covering RSA keypairs,
Ed25519 keypairs, multi-level dependencies, and complex nested objects
- docs/references.md: Updated publickey documentation to clarify
single-compile capability and added Ed25519 example

commit.message:
fix(vpn): use compat proxy for plain HTTP speedtest

Vision flow requires TLS Application Data after handshake and breaks when
forwarding plain HTTP traffic. Speedtest servers on port 8080 use plain HTTP,
causing 'cannot decrypt peer's message' errors.

Solution: Create proxy-compat (Reality without Vision flow) specifically for
speedtest plain HTTP traffic on port 8080, while keeping Vision for all HTTPS.

commit.message:
feat: detect environment URL confusion and suggest corrections (#97)

* feat: add task result retrieval for workflow executions

Add GET /platform/automation/v1/executions/{id}/tasks/{taskName}/result
support to surface the return value of individual tasks (e.g. the object
returned by a JavaScript task's default export function).

Changes:
- pkg/resources/workflow/execution.go: add GetTaskResult() method
- cmd/logs.go: add --result flag to 'logs wfe --task <name> --result'
- cmd/exec_workflows.go: add --show-results flag to 'exec workflow --wait'

Usage:
  # Fetch the return value of a specific task
  dtctl logs wfe <execution-id> --task <task-name> --result

  # Execute workflow and show all task return values on completion
  dtctl exec workflow <id> --wait --show-results

Fixes: without this, the only way to retrieve JavaScript task output was
to hit the automation REST API directly with curl/Bearer token.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor: move task result retrieval to 'results wfe' command

- Remove --result flag from 'logs wfe' (logs = stdout/stderr; results
  = structured return values — semantically distinct)
- Add new 'dtctl results workflow-execution' command (alias: wfe) with
  required --task flag and -o json/yaml output support
- Add --show-results flag to 'exec workflow --wait' for convenience
- Update QUICK_START.md with both new commands

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor: rename 'results wfe' to 'get wfe-task-result'

Per feedback in #71, move task result retrieval from the 'results' top-level
command group to 'get wfe-task-result', consistent with existing patterns
where 'get' is the verb for reading resources.

Before: dtctl results wfe <execution-id> --task <task-name>
After:  dtctl get wfe-task-result <execution-id> --task <task-name>
        dtctl get workflow-execution-task-result <execution-id> --task <task-name>

This mirrors the existing 'dtctl logs wfe' / 'dtctl get wfe' relationship
and maps directly to the API endpoint (.../result), leaving no ambiguity
between task results and task metadata.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* test: add golden tests and cmd-layer tests for wfe-task-result

- Add golden tests for TaskExecution list (table/json/yaml/csv) and
  single task result (json/yaml) using real production structs
- Add cmd-layer tests: full RunE execution with mock server,
  agent mode envelope verification, PreRunE validation
- Test execWorkflowAgent for success/no-wait/error state paths
- Fix help text grouping: place wfe-task-result near workflow-executions
  in both Long description and AddCommand ordering
- Add documents to resource list in get help text

* feat: detect environment URL confusion and suggest corrections

Users commonly confuse Dynatrace environment URL domains (e.g.,
live.dynatrace.com vs apps.dynatrace.com), leading to cryptic 403
errors. Add multi-layered URL confusion detection to catch and guide
users at multiple points:

- Core detection: pkg/diagnostic/urlcheck.go with CheckEnvironmentURL()
  and URLSuggestions() covering live.dynatrace.com, bare tenant domains,
  dev/sprint without .apps., and /e/ managed/ActiveGate paths
- Error-time hints: on 403/401/connectivity errors, append URL hints to
  both stderr output and agent-mode JSON envelope (cmd/root.go)
- Doctor command: new 'Environment URL' check step (cmd/doctor.go)
- Early warning: ctx set and auth login warn on stderr when environment
  URL has known problems (cmd/ctx.go, cmd/auth.go)

All warnings go to stderr, not stdout. The feature detects and suggests
corrections without blocking the user.

---------

Co-authored-by: David Haunschmied <david.haunschmied@dynatrace.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
